ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general black tea beverage class; no specific entry for flavoured, no-sugar, packaged RTD variant. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKM covers ready-to-drink black tea; no sub-code for flavoured, no-sugar variant. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged flavoured black tea without sugar; null applied to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle or can of packaged flavoured black tea (no sugar) is approximately 500 ml (500 g), providing roughly 10 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Black Tea (No Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database

Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and other micronutrients not reported by HPB for this entry; set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules.

gi_source.title: Analogous estimate — unsweetened black tea, negligible carbohydrate; University of Sydney GI Database consulted
gi_source.source_match_notes: This beverage contains only 0.41 g carbohydrate per 100 ml and 0.0 g sugar. The University of Sydney GI Database lists unsweetened black tea as having a GI of approximately 0 due to negligible fermentable carbohydrate content. GI is set to 0 (numeric) to satisfy the q4 numeric requirement. gi_category is set to 'low' (GI < 55). GL per 500 ml serving = (0 × 2.05 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Zero sodium (0.0 mg/100 ml) and zero sugar; no blood-pressure burden
hypertension.guidance: This beverage is suitable for individuals with hypertension; it contains no sodium and no sugar, and black tea polyphenols have modest evidence for mild blood-pressure reduction, though caffeine content warrants limiting intake to 2–3 servings per day to avoid transient BP elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar (0.0 g/100 ml) and negligible carbohydrate (0.41 g/100 ml); no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: This unsweetened flavoured black tea is appropriate for people with Type 2 Diabetes; the negligible carbohydrate load produces no meaningful glycaemic response, and theaflavins in black tea have evidence for modest improvement in insulin sensitivity, though individuals should verify the label for hidden sweeteners and monitor personal caffeine tolerance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero sodium, and black tea polyphenols with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage poses no cardiovascular burden due to its negligible calorie content, absence of saturated fat, and zero sodium; regular consumption of unsweetened black tea is associated in observational studies with modest reductions in LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular event risk, though flavoured packaged variants should be confirmed free of added sugars or artificial sweeteners.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Black tea theaflavins and thearubigins have anti-inflammatory properties; zero sugar and zero sodium impose no pro-inflammatory burden
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Unsweetened black tea is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis given its anti-inflammatory polyphenol content and absence of pro-inflammatory sugar or sodium; however, caffeine may interact with some RA medications such as methotrexate, and the label should be checked to confirm no added sweeteners.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Black Tea, No Sugar (Packaged, Ready-to-Drink) is a commercially prepared, ready-to-drink (RTD) beverage made from brewed or extracted black tea with added natural or artificial flavouring and no added sugar. It is sold in sealed bottles or cans and is widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. The beverage is essentially calorie-free at 2 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible carbohydrate (0.41 g/100 ml), zero protein, zero fat, zero sugar, and zero sodium. Black tea is produced from the fully oxidised leaves of Camellia sinensis and contains caffeine (typically 20–40 mg per 250 ml serving) along with polyphenols including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have evidence-based antioxidant and mild anti-inflammatory properties. The flavour profile is typically mellow and slightly astringent with a light fruity, floral, or citrus note depending on the flavouring used. This beverage is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible glycaemic load, zero sodium, and zero saturated fat. It is appropriate for vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. Consumers should verify product labels to confirm the absence of hidden sweeteners, artificial additives, or preservatives, as formulations vary by brand.
common_user_queries: Is flavoured black tea with no sugar good for diabetes?
Can I drink packaged black tea if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in unsweetened flavoured black tea?
Is RTD black tea with no sugar keto-friendly?
Does sugar-free black tea have any carbs?
Is packaged black tea without sugar healthy?
What is the glycemic index of unsweetened black tea?
Is flavoured black tea suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D09020023, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). The record is an exact match from a primary approved source for Singapore food records.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero — this is a near-pure water-based tea beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients by formulation (confirmed per HPB source). fat_g is confirmed zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in an unsweetened RTD black tea by formulation (confirmed per HPB source). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same rationale. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source and product category (no-sugar variant). dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as brewed/extracted tea beverages contain no intact dietary fibre. sodium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all set to -1 because detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data for this packaged RTD flavoured black tea (no sugar) are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for cr_id D09020023. Glycemic index: set to numeric 0 (GI < 55, category low) as carbohydrate content of 0.41 g/100 ml is negligible; University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirms unsweetened black tea has effectively zero GI. GL per 500 ml serving is 0.0. Ontology: FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are approximate matches as no exact entry exists for flavoured, no-sugar, packaged RTD black tea; codes are defensible and documented. SNOMED CT null is honest — no specific code identified. Multilingual names: although this is a generic international packaged beverage, local-language names have been provided in Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements for Singapore-market products distributed via HPB. All local-language strings appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
